data.name: label4.ToolTip
data.value: The 'step' column below sets the increment between successive h, k, l values in the test-mode scan (0.001-1; a value below 1 gives fractional indices).

data.name: numericBoxCutoffD.ToolTip
data.value: Smallest d-spacing to include in the reflection list. Reflections from planes closer together than this (which appear at high diffraction angles) are left out. Lowering the cutoff adds more, higher-angle reflections; raising it keeps only the low-angle ones. Unit: nm or Angstrom, matching the length-unit setting.

data.name: labelModel.ToolTip
data.value: Selects the formula (parameterization) used to compute the atomic scattering factor - how strongly a single atom scatters the beam as a function of angle. The available choices change with the beam: X-ray offers f(s) or F(q)+S(q); electron offers the Peng, Kirkland or 8-Gaussian models.

data.name: radioButtonElectronKirkland.ToolTip
data.value: Electron atomic scattering factor fe(s) using Kirkland's parameterization (3 Lorentzians plus 3 Gaussians). Often preferred for high-angle / high-resolution image-simulation work because it stays accurate out to large s.

data.name: numericBoxK_step.ToolTip
data.value: Step (increment) for the index k in the test-mode scan. Range 0.001-1; a value below 1 produces fractional indices.

data.name: numericBoxH_max.ToolTip
data.value: Highest value of the index h reached in the test-mode scan.

data.name: radioButtonElectronEightGaussian.ToolTip
data.value: Electron atomic scattering factor fe(s) from an 8-Gaussian fit. An alternative parameterization; compare it with Peng and Kirkland to see how much the choice of model affects the result.

data.name: radioButtonXrayFs.ToolTip
data.value: Plot the X-ray atomic scattering factor f(s) - the amplitude (in units of one electron) that a single atom scatters elastically. The horizontal axis is s = sin(theta)/lambda in 1/Angstrom, which increases with the diffraction angle. At s = 0 the value equals the atomic number Z (the total number of electrons); it falls off towards higher angle.

data.name: checkBoxHideProhibitedPlanes.ToolTip
data.value: Hide 'forbidden' reflections - those whose intensity is exactly zero because the waves scattered from different atoms cancel out. These systematic absences come from the crystal's symmetry (for example a body-centred or face-centred lattice). When unchecked, these zero-intensity reflections are also listed, with the extinction rule that suppresses them shown in its own column.

data.name: label5.ToolTip
data.value: Scan range of the index h in test mode: minimum, maximum and step.

data.name: numericBoxH_min.ToolTip
data.value: Lowest value of the index h in the test-mode scan (it runs from h min to h max in steps of h step).

data.name: buttonCopyClipboard.ToolTip
data.value: Copy the whole reflection table to the clipboard as tab-separated text (only the columns currently shown). You can paste it straight into Excel or a text editor.

data.name: numericBoxL_min.ToolTip
data.value: Lowest value of the index l in the test-mode scan (it runs from l min to l max in steps of l step).

data.name: waveLengthControl.ToolTip
data.value: プローブ(線源)とその波長・エネルギーを選びます。 プローブは X線・電子線・中性子から選べ、切り替えると原子の散乱の仕方や有効なタブが 変わります。 X線・中性子では波長 λ(または光子エネルギー)を、電子線では加速電圧を設定します。 波長は回折角を決め、この画面のすべての計算に使われます。

data.name: label1.ToolTip
data.value: The 'min' column below sets the lowest h, k and l index used in the test-mode scan.

data.name: numericBoxL_step.ToolTip
data.value: Step (increment) for the index l in the test-mode scan. Range 0.001-1; a value below 1 produces fractional indices.

data.name: label6.ToolTip
data.value: Scan range of the index k in test mode: minimum, maximum and step.

data.name: checkBoxDebyeWaller.ToolTip
data.value: Multiply the scattering-factor curves by the Debye-Waller factor exp(-B*s^2), which accounts for the thermal vibration of the atoms. Because vibrating atoms are smeared out, the apparent scattering power drops faster at high angle. B is the isotropic displacement parameter (Biso) taken from the crystal data; with this off, the atoms are treated as stationary.

data.name: numericBoxL_max.ToolTip
data.value: Highest value of the index l reached in the test-mode scan.

data.name: checkBoxTest.ToolTip
data.value: Turn on test mode. This reveals a panel where you can scan an arbitrary range of h, k, l indices - including non-integer (fractional) values - instead of only the physically allowed reflections. Useful for checking how the structure factor behaves; leave it off for normal use.

data.name: numericBoxK_min.ToolTip
data.value: Lowest value of the index k in the test-mode scan (it runs from k min to k max in steps of k step).

data.name: numericBoxH_step.ToolTip
data.value: Step (increment) for the index h in the test-mode scan. Range 0.001-1; a value below 1 produces fractional indices.

data.name: checkBoxBragBrentano.ToolTip
data.value: Show intensities as they would appear in a normal powder X-ray diffractometer (Bragg-Brentano focusing geometry). This multiplies the structure-factor intensity by the angle-dependent Lorentz-polarization factor and the multiplicity, so the relative peak heights match a real powder pattern rather than the raw |F| squared. Forbidden and equivalent reflections are hidden automatically in this mode.

data.name: label7.ToolTip
data.value: Scan range of the index l in test mode: minimum, maximum and step.

data.name: radioButtonXrayFqSq.ToolTip
data.value: Plot two X-ray quantities from xraylib versus q = sin(theta)/lambda: F(q) - the coherent (elastic) form factor, drawn as the bold curve. S(q) - the incoherent (Compton) scattering function, drawn pale. Together they show how scattering shifts from coherent at low angle to incoherent at high angle. Disabled when xraylib is unavailable.

data.name: checkBoxHideEquivalentPlane.ToolTip
data.value: Hide symmetry-equivalent reflections. The crystal's symmetry makes several sets of planes physically identical - for example (100), (010) and (001) in a cubic crystal - so they give exactly the same intensity. When checked, only one representative of each such group is shown, together with its multiplicity (how many equivalent planes it stands for).

data.name: numericBoxAttenThickness.ToolTip
data.value: Sample thickness used for the X-ray transmission calculation T = exp(-mu*t), where mu is the linear attenuation coefficient and t this thickness. It sets the 'Transmission (t = ...)' value in the table and the transmission-versus- energy curve. Unit: micrometres (um).

data.name: radioButtonElectronPeng.ToolTip
data.value: Electron atomic scattering factor fe(s) using Peng's parameterization (a sum of 5 Gaussians). This is the default and a good general choice. For electrons fe(s) is related to the electrostatic potential of the atom; its unit here is nm.

data.name: numericBoxK_max.ToolTip
data.value: Highest value of the index k reached in the test-mode scan.

data.name: label3.ToolTip
data.value: The 'max' column below sets the highest h, k and l index used in the test-mode scan.

data.name: dataGridView.ToolTip
data.value: Table of all diffraction reflections for the current crystal and radiation. Each row is one family of lattice planes, named by its Miller indices (h k l). Columns include the multiplicity (number of symmetry-equivalent planes), the d-spacing (distance between the planes), the diffraction angle 2-theta, the structure factor (the wave amplitude scattered by one unit cell), the relative intensity, and any extinction (reflection-forbidding) rule. Use the check boxes on the left to hide symmetry-equivalent or forbidden reflections.

data.name: Graph.Title.FqSq
data.value: F(q) coherent and S(q) incoherent scattering

data.name: Graph.Title.NeutronScatteringNoGraph
data.value: Neutron scattering length b does not depend on s (no curve)

data.name: Graph.Title.ElectronTransportNormalized
data.value: Electron transport (normalized: σ, elastic MFP, dE/ds)

data.name: Graph.Title.NeutronNoEnergyGraph
data.value: Neutron cross sections are listed in the table (no energy graph)
